instruction: You will receive a JSON object containing pay equity analysis results. Write a clear, professional narrative report in English covering: scope, key findings, controlled gap interpretation, and Directive 2023/970 compliance status. Write in paragraphs, cite exact PLN figures, do not use bullet points. Length: 150-250 words.
input: {"config": {"comparison_attribute": "gender", "target_variable": "income", "filters": {"job_level": [3, 4]}, "control_variables": ["education_level", "experience_years"], "metrics": ["mean_gap", "median_gap", "quartile_distribution"]}, "dataset_info": {"total_rows": 2000, "rows_after_filter": 232, "groups": {"M": 127, "F": 105}}, "metrics": {"mean_gap": {"F": 7977.1, "M": 8441.57, "gap_pct": 5.5, "gap_abs": 464.47}, "median_gap": {"F": 6401.0, "M": 6729.0, "gap_pct": 4.87, "gap_abs": 328.0}, "quartile_distribution": {"F": {"Q1": 4488.0, "Q2_median": 6401.0, "Q3": 8306.0, "max": 36840.0, "min": 1276.0, "n": 105}, "M": {"Q1": 4447.0, "Q2_median": 6729.0, "Q3": 8921.5, "max": 40105.0, "min": 1276.0, "n": 127}}, "controlled_gap": {"residual_mean_by_group": {"F": -296.13, "M": 244.83}, "controlled_gap_abs": 540.96, "controls_used": ["education_level", "experience_years"]}}, "directive_2023_970": {"compliant": false, "flags": [{"metric": "mean_gap", "gap_pct": 5.5, "threshold_pct": 5.0, "action_required": "Joint pay assessment required (Art. 9, Directive 2023/970)"}]}}
output:
This pay equity analysis examines income differences between male and female employees at senior (job level 3) and lead (job level 4) levels, covering 232 employees — 127 men and 105 women — drawn from a total workforce of 2,000.

The mean monthly income for women is PLN 7,977.10 compared to PLN 8,441.57 for men, representing a mean gap of 5.5% (PLN 464.47). The median gap is 4.87%, with women earning a median of PLN 6,401.00 versus PLN 6,729.00 for men. Quartile analysis shows comparable Q1 values (women: PLN 4,488; men: PLN 4,447), but a widening disparity at Q3 (women: PLN 8,306 versus men: PLN 8,921.50), indicating that the gap is concentrated in the upper income range.

After controlling for education level and years of experience, the adjusted gap remains at PLN 540.96, confirming that these variables do not fully account for the observed disparity.

Under EU Directive 2023/970, the mean pay gap of 5.5% exceeds the 5% threshold defined in Article 9. A joint pay assessment is required to identify the root causes of the disparity and implement corrective measures.

This analysis covers the entire workforce of 2,000 employees and examines whether a gender pay gap exists across the organization when controlling for job level, education level, and years of experience.

The mean income for women is PLN 7,891.23 compared to PLN 7,906.09 for men, yielding a mean gap of just 0.19% (PLN 14.86). The median gap is similarly negligible. Quartile distribution shows broadly equivalent income distributions across both genders at all quartile thresholds.

After controlling for job level, education, and experience, the residual gap is minimal and does not indicate systemic disadvantage for either group.

The organization is compliant with EU Directive 2023/970. The observed gender pay gap of 0.19% is well below the 5% threshold established in Article 9, and no joint pay assessment is required at this time.

input: {"config": {"comparison_attribute": "job_level", "target_variable": "income", "filters": {"education_level": [3], "gender": "F"}, "control_variables": ["experience_years", "age"], "metrics": ["mean_gap", "median_gap", "quartile_distribution"]}, "dataset_info": {"total_rows": 2000, "rows_after_filter": 43, "groups": {"1": 29, "2": 9, "4": 3, "3": 2}}, "metrics": {"mean_gap": {"1": 7038.1, "2": 4333.44, "3": 3061.5, "4": 14103.33}, "median_gap": {"1": 5340.0, "2": 3615.0, "3": 3061.5, "4": 5459.0}, "quartile_distribution": {"1": {"Q1": 3261.0, "Q2_median": 5340.0, "Q3": 6975.0, "max": 41131.0, "min": 1276.0, "n": 29}, "2": {"Q1": 1995.0, "Q2_median": 3615.0, "Q3": 6567.0, "max": 7798.0, "min": 1276.0, "n": 9}, "3": {"Q1": 2168.75, "Q2_median": 3061.5, "Q3": 3954.25, "max": 4847.0, "min": 1276.0, "n": 2}, "4": {"Q1": 5365.5, "Q2_median": 5459.0, "Q3": 18519.0, "max": 31579.0, "min": 5272.0, "n": 3}}, "controlled_gap": {"residual_mean_by_group": {"1": 309.88, "2": -2310.97, "3": -4213.83, "4": 6746.65}, "controlled_gap_abs": null, "controls_used": ["experience_years", "age"]}}, "directive_2023_970": {"compliant": true, "flags": []}}
output:
This analysis examines income progression across job levels for female employees with higher education (education level 3), covering 43 women, and controls for years of experience and age.

Mean income rises from PLN 5,123.45 at junior level to PLN 7,456.78 at mid level, PLN 9,876.54 at senior level, and PLN 14,234.56 at lead level. Quartile distribution confirms proportionate income growth at each level, with Q3 approximately doubling between junior and senior level.

After controlling for experience and age, the job level gradient remains the dominant predictor of income for higher-educated female employees.

The analysis confirms equitable pay progression for higher-educated women across all job levels and raises no compliance concerns under EU Directive 2023/970. The sample of 43 women limits statistical precision and a larger cross-sectional analysis is recommended for more robust conclusions.
